repo.name: open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector
repo.scope: the OpenTelemetry Collector (Go, multi-module telemetry pipeline)

reference_claims.text: There is a Receiver component type (with a receiver Factory).
There is a Processor component type (with a processor Factory).
There is an Exporter component type (with an exporter Factory).
There is a Connector component type (with a connector Factory).
There is an Extension component type.
There is a Service component that initializes telemetry and the pipelines.
The pipelines are represented by an internal pipeline Graph.
There is a Collector type that owns the run lifecycle.
There is a pdata package that defines the internal pipeline data model.
There is a consumer package defining the Consumer interface (the data sink contract between components).
A Receiver is how data gets into the Collector: it accepts data in some format, translates it into the internal format, and passes it on to processors and exporters.
An Exporter is how data leaves the Collector (sends data to a destination/backend).
A Processor processes/transforms telemetry data as it passes through a pipeline (between receivers and exporters).
A Connector joins two pipelines: it consumes data as an exporter at the end of one pipeline and emits data as a receiver at the start of another.
An Extension provides capabilities that are not part of pipeline data processing; extensions are started/stopped and notified about the Collector configuration and when the pipeline is ready.
The Service initializes telemetry and then initializes/builds the pipelines.
The Graph converts the configured pipelines into an internal graph representation (building nodes and edges between components).
The Collector is created by NewCollector; Collector.Run starts the collector and blocks until it shuts down.
Within a pipeline, data flows from a receiver through processor(s) to an exporter.
A receiver is given a handle to a nextConsumer, representing where it sends its data next in the telemetry pipeline.
A Connector bridges two pipelines, acting as both an exporter (consuming) and a receiver (emitting).
Each component type contains a Factory interface and a corresponding NewFactory function, used by implementations to register their key functions with the Collector.
The pipeline graph is topologically sorted, and components are created and started in reverse topological order (Graph.StartAll calls Start on each component in reverse topological order).
Components (receivers, processors, exporters, extensions, connectors) MUST use lower_snake_case names.
The Collector does not crash or exit outside main() (no log.Fatal/os.Exit after startup); errors are propagated to the caller, and it does not crash on bad input in receivers or after startup.
The codebase is organized as multiple Go modules (per-component / per-package modules), each with its own go.mod.
Service.Start runs in order: start all extensions, notify extensions about the configuration, start all pipelines, then notify extensions that the pipeline is ready.
The startup path is command.NewCommand -> NewCollector -> Collector.Run -> setupConfigurationComponents -> Service.New/Service.Start.
The Collector uses the zap structured logger for logging (and avoids per-data-item logging).

emitted_claims.text: receiver is a component: the pipeline ingress module defining Traces/Metrics/Logs/Profiles interfaces + Factory.
receiver role = pipeline ingress: translates external-format data into internal pdata and pushes it to the next consumer.
processor is a component: pipeline-middle module consuming one signal, optionally transforming it, forwarding to the next consumer.
processor role = pipeline-middle: consumes one signal, optionally transforms it, forwards to the next consumer.
exporter is a component: the egress tier / terminal pipeline component.
exporter role = egress: terminal pipeline component that consumes telemetry and sends it to an external destination.
connector is a component: joins pipelines, acting as an exporter from one pipeline and a receiver to downstream pipelines.
connector role = joins pipelines: acts as an exporter from one pipeline and a receiver to one-or-more downstream pipelines.
service is a component: the runtime orchestrator + graph builder.
service role = runtime orchestrator + graph builder: assembles the pipeline DAG, owns component lifecycle, is the concrete component.Host.
otelcol is a component: the collector entry / CLI + lifecycle layer.
otelcol role = collector entry: CLI + lifecycle; Collector.Run starts the collector and blocks until shutdown.
pdata is a component: the canonical in-memory data model for all pipeline signals + proto/JSON (un)marshalers.
pdata role = data model: canonical in-memory data model for all pipeline signals (traces/metrics/logs/profiles + shared pcommon).
consumer is a component: defines the per-signal Traces/Metrics/Logs (+experimental Profiles) consumer contracts + BaseConsumer.Capabilities().
consumer role = signal interfaces: per-signal consumer contracts that receive pipeline data and forward it on.
component is a component: defines the foundational lifecycle interface + shared ID/Type/Kind/Config/Host contracts.
component role = the foundational Component lifecycle interface (Start/Shutdown) that every receiver/exporter/processor/connector/extension must implement.
extension is a component kind wired into the service pipeline (alongside receiver/processor/exporter/connector).
otelcol is a Go multi-module project implementing an OpenTelemetry-style telemetry collector.
The system is a strictly layered dependency DAG built from sealed-factory component contracts; component is the base layer depended-ON, pdata is the leaf/foundation.
Each component is its own Go module wired to siblings via local replace directives, so the build manifest (go.mod) is the dependency-DAG source of truth.
Modules are go 1.25.0.
exporter/ ships 8 Go modules.
consumer/ ships 5 independent Go modules (consumer, xconsumer, consumererror, consumererror/xconsumererror, consumertest).
component/ ships 3 independent Go modules (component, componentstatus, componenttest).
pdata is several modules (pdata, pprofile, xpdata, testdata).
The reference OTLP receiver is a gRPC + HTTP receiver that multiplexes 4 signals over 2 transports from one shared instance.
Default OTLP endpoints are gRPC localhost:4317 and HTTP localhost:4318.
OTLP HTTP paths are /v1/traces, /v1/metrics, /v1/logs, and profiles /v1development/profiles.
In-memory pdata is the universal signal carrier (no on-the-wire intermediate store inside the pipeline).
otelcol CLI/runtime stack uses cobra CLI, zap/zapcore logging, confmap+koanf config, multierr error accumulation.
The pipeline DAG is built on the gonum graph library (gonum graph/simple + topo), not a hand-rolled graph.
pdata uses json-iterator/go and google.golang.org/protobuf for codecs and google.golang.org/grpc for OTLP request/response types.
Stability status: package class pkg, distributions core+contrib; signals beta (metrics/traces/logs), profiles alpha.
Stable consumer modules are v1.60.0; experimental/profile modules are v0.154.0.
receiver -> component (import / interface-embed: every receiver interface embeds component.Component).
receiver -> consumer (import / runtime-call: next sink, factory create-funcs receive downstream consumer.Traces/Metrics/Logs).
receiver -> pipeline (import: sentinel-error pipeline.ErrSignalNotSupported + Signal switch).
receiver -> pdata (import: otlp wire types via otlpreceiver).
receiver -> consumer/xconsumer (import xconsumer.Profiles, experimental signal).
receiver -> component (project-ref: go.mod replace ../component).
receiver -> internal/componentalias (factory delegates ValidateComponentType / NewTypeAliasHolder).
receiver -> consumer/consumererror (receiverhelper uses consumererror.IsDownstream to classify refused vs failed).
receiver -> internal/sharedcomponent (otlpreceiver per-config singleton map).
receiver -> config/confighttp + config/configgrpc (otlpreceiver server transport construction).
receiver has NO edge to processor/exporter/connector/service/otelcol ג€” none in go.mod and none imported; it sits strictly below them.
processor -> consumer (import / interface-embed: processor signal interfaces ARE consumers).
processor -> consumer (runtime-call: next consumer forwarding target in Factory.Create*).
processor -> component (import / interface-embed).
processor -> pipeline (import: ErrSignalNotSupported sentinel).
processor -> consumer/xconsumer (import xconsumer.Profiles in xprocessor).
processor -> pdata (import ptrace/pmetric/plog in processorhelper + batchprocessor).
processor -> consumer/consumererror (permanent error in batchprocessor).
processor -> component/client (client.Info/Metadata context for batch sharding).
processor -> internal/memorylimiter (memorylimiterprocessor delegates admission control to shared memorylimiter.MemoryLimiter).
exporter -> component (import / interface-embed / project-ref).
exporter -> consumer (import / interface-embed ג€” exporter IS a consumer / project-ref).
exporter -> pipeline (import / runtime-call ErrSignalNotSupported, Signal key / project-ref).
exporter -> pdata (marshal ptrace/pmetric/plog/pprofile to OTLP / project-ref).
exporter -> extension (runtime-call host.GetExtensions -> storage.Extension -> storage.Client / project-ref).
exporter -> receiver (project-ref, local replace, build-graph edge; used by exporterhelper test rigs).
exporter -> internal/componentalias (componentalias.ValidateComponentType before each Create).
exporter -> config/configgrpc + config/configretry + config/configoptional (gRPC ClientConn dial, retry/backoff, optional queue config).
connector -> consumer (import / interface-embed; routers typed over consumer types).
connector -> component (import / interface-embed component.Component/Factory/ID/TelemetrySettings/BuildInfo).
connector -> pdata (project-ref; data model carried via consumers).
connector -> pipeline (routers key by pipeline.ID; errors cite pipeline.Signal*).
connector -> internal/fanoutconsumer (routers build fan-out via fanoutconsumer.NewLogs/NewMetrics/NewTraces).
connector -> internal/componentalias (TypeAliasHolder + ValidateComponentType in every Create*).
connector -> consumer/xconsumer (xconnector profiles surface typed over xconsumer.Profiles).
connector -> pipeline/xpipeline (xconnector error paths cite xpipeline.SignalProfiles).
connector has NO edge to receiver/exporter/service/otelcol; its exporter+receiver duality is interface composition (consumer embedding) only.
service -> receiver (import; stores ReceiversFactories map).
service -> processor (import; ProcessorsFactories map).
service -> exporter (import; ExportersFactories map).
service -> connector (import; ConnectorsFactories map).
service -> extension (import; ExtensionsFactories map).
service -> component (import + runtime-call node.(component.Component).Start/Shutdown).
service -> consumer (import + runtime-wiring consumer.Traces/Metrics/Logs + Capabilities).
service -> pdata (import pcommon.NewResource).
service -> otelcol (project-ref only: go.mod require + local replace ../otelcol; NO source import in this component).
service -> confmap (config substrate: CollectorConf *confmap.Conf, extensions notified with cloned confmap.Conf).
service -> pipeline (signal/ID types; graph keys nodes by pipeline.ID/pipeline.Signal).
otelcol -> service (runtime-call: constructs and owns the running service via service.New).
otelcol -> service (import).
otelcol -> component (import).
otelcol -> receiver (import; Factories.Receivers map).
otelcol -> processor (import; Factories.Processors map).
otelcol -> exporter (import; Factories.Exporters map).
otelcol -> extension (import; Factories.Extensions map).
otelcol -> connector (import; Factories.Connectors map).
otelcol -> consumer (project-ref; transitive via service/pipeline graph).
otelcol -> pdata (project-ref / indirect require; transitive via service/consumer).
otelcol -> confmap (config resolution via confmap.Resolver, confmap.Validate, confmap.New().Marshal).
otelcol -> featuregate (the featuregate subcommand + global registry flag wiring).
otelcol -> service/telemetry (Factories.Telemetry telemetry.Factory; nil-telemetry is a hard error).
pdata -> featuregate (import; registers pdata.useProtoPooling gate) ג€” the only production cross-component edge.
pdata -> client (import; xpdata sub-module only, NOT the root module; encodes/decodes request context).
consumer -> pdata (import signal payload types plog.Logs/ptrace.Traces/pmetric.Metrics).
consumer -> pdata (project-ref, go.mod require + local replace ../pdata).
consumer -> pdata/pprofile (experimental xconsumer.Profiles typed on pprofile.Profiles).
consumer -> external grpc (consumererror.Error carries *grpc/status.Status; maps OTLP gRPC/HTTP codes to retryability).
component -> pdata (import pcommon type-ref: TelemetrySettings.Resource pcommon.Resource).
component -> pdata (project-ref, direct require + replace => ../pdata).
component -> pipeline (componentstatus.InstanceID encodes pipeline.ID).
component has no outgoing edges to receiver/processor/exporter/connector/service/otelcol/consumer; its only non-test collector imports are pdata + pipeline.
pdata is the leaf/foundation: no production source imports receiver/processor/exporter/connector/service/otelcol/consumer/component.
Pipeline graph MUST be a DAG: service builds it on gonum and fails with 'cycle detected' via topo.DirectedCyclesIn, rendering the cycle starting from a connector.
Sealed factories: Receiver/Processor/Exporter/Connector Factory interfaces cannot be implemented directly ג€” the unexported unexportedFactoryFunc() marker forces construction through NewFactory.
Unsupported signals MUST return pipeline.ErrSignalNotSupported ג€” the default when no WithTraces/WithMetrics/WithLogs option is given.
Receive-then-acknowledge ordering invariant (receiver): receive -> push via nextConsumer.Consume*() -> only then ack/fail; storage-extension checkpoints stored only AFTER Consume*() returns.
Post-return ownership transfer (consumer): after Consume* returns, the payload is no longer accessible ג€” accessing it is undefined behavior.
A processor MUST NOT call the next component on error ג€” on error the returned data is ignored.
MutatesData copy-on-mutate contract: a processor that modifies input MUST set Capabilities.MutatesData=true (default non-mutating via NewBaseImpl).
The service capabilities node aggregates the MutatesData flag across all processors + the exporter fanout so receivers know whether to clone data.
processorhelper's own default capability is MutatesData: true (processors are assumed to mutate unless WithCapabilities overrides).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md / Critical to know + pdata invariants
emitted_claims.text: pdata mutability invariant: every wrapper carries a *State; mutating methods call AssertMutable() which panics 'invalid access to shared data' when read-only; MarkReadOnly() flips the bit.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md / Critical to know + pdata invariants
emitted_claims.text: pdata State holds an atomic ref-count; Unref()==true means releasable; a negative count panics 'Cannot unref freed data'.
Experimental signals (Profiles) are segregated into separate x* modules/packages that wrap the stable factory, keeping the stable interfaces signal-frozen.

No persistent application store except the exporter send queue; the exporter persistent queue is a durable FIFO backed by a storage.Client from a host-mediated storage extension; default queue is in-memory; all other component state is in-memory only.
There is no application database; telemetry data is carried in-memory as pdata OTLP proto structs serialized by hand-written varint codecs.
pdata optional sync.Pool memory pools for proto value structs, gated by the pdata.useProtoPooling alpha gate.
pdata serialized output forms: protobuf bytes via ProtoMarshaler/ProtoUnmarshaler and JSON via in-house streaming codec internal/json.
Exporter in-memory send queue (default): QueueBatchConfig stores 1000 requests, non-blocking when full, ErrQueueIsFull on overflow.
Exporter external backend sink (not a local store): OTLP gRPC ClientConn + clients; otlphttp *http.Client.
The exporter persistent queue uses metadata key qmv0 (legacy ri/wi/di), keyed component.KindExporter when fetching its storage client.
receiver owns no store: an in-process per-config singleton registry (sharedcomponent.NewMap) dedups one otlpReceiver per config; emits accepted/refused/failed telemetry counters.
processor owns in-memory batch buffers only (batchprocessor pending-batch buffer per shard via MoveAndAppendTo, flushed by size/timeout); no persistence.
processor metadata-keyed shard map multiShardBatcher.batchers (sync.Map keyed by attribute set), guarded by mutex + size counter.
connector has no persistence; state is in-memory only ג€” BaseRouter holds Consumers map[pipeline.ID]T (copied via maps.Copy) and a fanout func.
service owns no persistent store: in-memory pipeline DAG componentGraph *simple.DirectedGraph (gonum) plus pipelines and instanceIDs maps.
service reads the collector config object in memory (CollectorConf *confmap.Conf); it does not load config from disk itself.
otelcol in-memory zap log buffer (bufferedCore) captures early logs then drains once via TakeLogs().
otelcol read-only config source resolved/watched/re-read via confmap.Resolver behind ConfigProvider; never written back.
consumer has no production persistence; the only data-holding type is the in-memory test sink consumertest.TracesSink/MetricsSink/LogsSink/ProfilesSink (mutex-guarded slices for tests).
component declares no data stores ג€” contract layer only; the only state types are in-memory value structs (BuildInfo, TelemetrySettings, componentstatus.Event).
Strict layered intra-pipeline flow: receiver -> capabilities node -> processors (ordered) -> fanout node -> exporters; edges drawn only in that direction.
A fanout node is ALWAYS inserted before exporters, acting as a noop even with a single exporter.
Receiver and exporter nodes are deduplicated across same-signal pipelines (node ID = pipeline-type + component-ID).
Component-type/ID consistency gate is checked on every create via componentalias.ValidateComponentType(f, set.ID).
A connector's 9 (3x3) Create* signal-pair methods enforce only-supported conversions, else return ErrDataTypes/ErrSignalNotSupported.
Functional-options factory pattern across the kinds: NewFactory(type, defaultCfg, ...FactoryOption); WithX options install a Create* func + StabilityLevel.
Public structs use the unkeyed-literal guard ג€” a trailing _ struct{} field forbids unkeyed-literal initialization (forward-compat).
internal/ packages are import-restricted to their module by Go's internal-package rule; pdata's raw OTLP structs/codecs live under pdata/internal, exposed only via opaque value types.
ErrSkipProcessingData drops data WITHOUT propagating an error up the pipeline.
pdata Value model is a typed tagged-union over OTLP AnyValue: ValueType has 8 variants Empty/Str/Int/Double/Bool/Map/Slice/Bytes; Value is pass-by-value over an internal pointer.
pdata model is machine-generated via mdatagen (//go:generate mdatagen metadata.yaml) ג€” edit templates/metadata, not the generated_* files.
pdata uses hand-rolled codecs (custom varint encode + custom JSON iterator/stream), not protobuf-go reflection ג€” a performance choice.
pprofile resolves string_value_ref/key_ref indices to real strings after unmarshal (Profiles dictionary indirection).
Lifecycle ordering is inverted vs topology: start in REVERSE toposort order, shutdown in FORWARD toposort order.
Fixed service start sequence: start extensions -> NotifyConfig -> start pipelines -> NotifyPipelineReady; shutdown is the reverse.
Telemetry providers shut down in reverse creation order (tracer, meter, logger) because tracer/meter may use the logger; Resource is created FIRST so providers share one service.instance.id.
Internal telemetry factory is mandatory ג€” New errors 'telemetry factory not provided' if nil.
Validate is a config-only dry run ג€” builds the graph with noop providers and discards the result.
Fatal component status (StatusFatalError) is pushed onto the host's AsyncErrorChannel chan error to signal the collector to abort.
Process metrics OS allow-list: registered only on linux/darwin/windows/freebsd/openbsd/solaris/plan9; other OSes continue with a warning instead of failing.
Three feature gates govern service behavior: service.AllowNoPipelines, service.profilesSupport, telemetry.newPipelineTelemetry.
Connector cross-pipeline validity is enforced: a connector used as exporter must have a supported corresponding receiver use (and vice-versa) or createNodes errors out.
Extension start order is computed (dependency-ordered) and stop order is its reverse (slices.Backward).
service serves zPages debug HTTP endpoints (servicez, pipelinez, extensionz, featurez) on an http.ServeMux.
otelcol lifecycle state machine Starting->Running->Closing->Closed stored atomically in atomic.Int64.

Single-select control loop multiplexes config-watch reload, async fatal error, OS signals (SIGHUP=reload, SIGINT/SIGTERM=graceful shutdown), shutdown chan, ctx cancel; hot reload rebuilds a fresh service.
Factory-mediated boundary: otelcol references only Factory interfaces keyed by component.Type, never concrete impls.
Config-reference integrity gate: every pipeline component reference and service extension must be a configured top-level component.
A connector ID may not collide with an exporter or receiver ID.
At least one receiver and one exporter are required unless AllowNoPipelines (no default component rule).
Redaction boundary: print-config defaults to redacted; unredacted is explicit and feature-gated behind otelcol.printInitialConfig.
otelcol telemetry factory mandatory ג€” unmarshal fails fast with errNilTelemetryFactory if nil.
CLI is cobra-based with subcommands featuregate/components/validate/print-config; --config takes repeatable URIs and --set rewrites dotted keys to :: YAML overrides (default scheme env).
Two-phase logging via swappable zap core: a bufferedCore captures early logs, wrapped by a collectorCore whose delegate is hot-swapped (atomic.Pointer) to the real service logger once it exists.
Config is a bare alias type Config any ג€” no compile-time constraint; the struct/tag contract is enforced only at test time by reflection in CheckConfigStruct.
Kind is a closed set of five sealed values (Receiver/Processor/Exporter/Extension/Connector) using an unexported-field struct so callers cannot mint new kinds.
Component ID is the unique type[/name] key (separator /); type regex ^[a-zA-Z][0-9a-zA-Z_]{0,62}$, name 1-1024 Unicode chars excluding whitespace/control/symbol.
Shutdown re-entrancy invariant: MUST be safe to call without a prior Start() and when already shut down; StartFunc/ShutdownFunc adapters are nil-safe.
Host-communication boundary: a Component reaches its host only via the Host interface and must type-assert + error if a required extra interface is absent.
componentstatus.InstanceID encodes its pipeline IDs as a single 0x20-delimited string specifically to stay Comparable / map-key usable.
StabilityLevel is an ordered enum starting at 1 (0 = Undefined skipped) with text (un)marshal + per-level log messages.

componentstatus.Status is an 8-value runtime health enum (None/Starting/OK/RecoverableError/PermanentError/FatalError/Stopping/Stopped); ReportStatus is best-effort and no-ops if host is not a Reporter.
OTLP error taxonomy lives in consumererror: permanent, Error (HTTP/gRPC status + retryable), downstreamError; retryability derived from OTLP HTTP codes (429/502/503/504) and gRPC codes.
Panic-on-success-code guard: NewOTLPHTTPError panics on a 2xx code and NewOTLPGRPCError panics on codes.OK.
consumertest.Consumer is a non-implementable convenience interface ג€” it carries an unexported unexported() method so external packages cannot implement it.

OTLP receiver multiplexes 4 signals over 2 transports from ONE shared instance; consumers registered lazily via registerXConsumer and a server is only started for a signal whose next consumer is non-nil.
receiver error-to-source mapping is protocol-dependent: a transport translates the Consume error into a protocol status (OTLP/HTTP codes; gRPC non-permanent->Unavailable, permanent->InvalidArgument).
exporterhelper is the reusable resilience toolkit: configurable timeout (default 5s), retry/backoff (disabled by default), queue+batch, capabilities, start/shutdown, assembled via functional Options.

OTLP egress retry classification by gRPC status: retries on Canceled/DeadlineExceeded/Aborted/OutOfRange/Unavailable/DataLoss (ResourceExhausted only with RetryInfo); marks non-retryable errors consumererror.NewPermanent.
OTLP partial-success handling: push paths log a warning with rejected-span/datapoint/log/profile counts instead of failing the whole request.
gRPC client construction is deferred to Start (not newExporter) because only there is the host available to build the auth round-tripper from Extensions.
Bundled reference exporters ship in-tree: otlp (gRPC), otlphttp (HTTP), debug (otlptext console dump), nop.
Storage access is host-mediated: the exporter queue never opens files itself; it must obtain a storage extension via host.GetExtensions(), erroring errNoStorageClient/errWrongExtensionType otherwise.
Batch processor flushes on SendBatchSize reached OR Timeout elapsed (defaults 8192 / 200ms), with per-shard goroutine + timer loops.
Memory-limiter processor is a pure admission gate: on MustRefuse() it returns the data unchanged plus ErrDataRefused; it never transforms payload.
processorhelper wraps a per-signal Process*Func into a consumer.New*, auto-recording in/out item counts + internal duration as OTel telemetry around each call.
BaseRouter[T any] is a Go generic shared across all four signal routers (logs/metrics/traces/profiles), parameterizing Consumer(...pipeline.ID) and PipelineIDs() over the consumer type.
A router feeds the FIRST consumer of each named pipeline and fans out across all pipelines; Consumer(ids...) builds a fan-out and returns an aggregated multierr error if any pipeline ID is missing.
Bundled forwardconnector is the canonical reference connector: a pass-through identity that declares MutatesData: false.
receiver/doc.go and pdata/doc.go are in-source package documentation (.go files), so their stated contracts are code-grounded, not removed prose.

Referent file: extension/extensioncapabilities/interfaces.go:28 (+ extension/extension.go).</referent>
<parameter name="evidence">RC-015's PRIMARY fact is the extension's defining role: capabilities outside pipeline data processing, plus config/pipeline-ready notification contracts (PipelineWatcher.Ready + ConfigWatcher.NotifyConfig at extension/extensioncapabilities/interfaces.go:23-49). The blind artifact has NO dedicated extension component doc (the only component kind missing one) and the "capabilities" mentions in it all refer to the service MutatesData capabilities node ג€” unrelated. scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): a key-reader + an EC-extractor (BLIND to the key) + one matcher per RC + a faithfulness refuter per overlap (vs CODE) + a beyond-doc finder. Workflow  (fact-keyed).
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session authored the curator keys, so it is not a fully fresh scorer; but every EC-extraction, match, and faithfulness JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent (the EC-extractor blind to the key). Recorded honestly.
scoring_provenance.reconciliation: RC-015 anti-vacuity corrected to read=True (extension package in read-set + referenced in blind output; genuine role MISS — the blind planner omitted extension as a first-class component despite RC-005 component-exists matching).

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
training-contamination — otelcol is a public repo; the blind output may be informed by pretraining, so the number is INDICATIVE.
